docs.text: After Workshop 1 executes, the team's sole objective is graph purity. See docs/design/11-graph-purity-tech-debt.md.

docs.title: Hard rule: no new logic leaks (enforced now, permanently)
docs.text:
A PR introduces a logic leak if it contains ANY of: - time.Now() or time.Since() called outside a CRD status write - An external HTTP call (GitHub API, Prometheus, webhook) in a reconciler's hot path - A cross-CRD status mutation (reconciler for CRD A writing to CRD B's status) - A decision whose result is NOT written to the reconciler's own CRD status - pkg/cel imported outside pkg/reconciler/policygate - An in-memory struct passing state between reconcile iterations - exec.Command() or os.Exec() in a reconciler

QA must block such PRs immediately with [NEEDS HUMAN]. The engineer must escalate and get explicit human approval before any such pattern is merged. This is not negotiable. There are no exceptions beyond the documented transitional workaround in docs/design/10-graph-first-architecture.md.

docs.title: How to Use This Document
docs.text:
Engineers: Before writing a single line of code, read the journey your feature contributes to. Understand what the user will type and what they expect to see. Build backwards from that. Also read docs/design/11-graph-purity-tech-debt.md — if your feature touches a package with known leaks, do not add new ones.

QA: After every PR, ask: "Does this bring us closer to passing the journeys below?" If a PR passes unit tests but moves us away from a journey, it fails QA. Also check: does this PR introduce any of the logic leak patterns listed above? If yes: block.

Coordinator: After each batch completes, verify which journeys now pass end-to-end. Update the journey status table at the bottom of this file.

docs.title: Journey 1: Quickstart — First Promotion in 15 Minutes
docs.text:
Source: docs/quickstart.md, examples/quickstart/

The user story: A platform engineer installs kardinal-promoter on a kind cluster, applies a 15-line Pipeline CRD, creates a Bundle, and watches it promote through test → uat → prod automatically, with a PR opened for prod that they review and merge.

docs.title: Exact steps that must work
docs.text:
# 1. Install
helm install kardinal oci://ghcr.io/pnz1990/kardinal-promoter/chart \
  --namespace kardinal-system --create-namespace

# 2. Verify
kardinal version
# must print: CLI: v0.1.x, Controller: v0.1.x

# 3. Create git credentials
kubectl create secret generic github-token \
  --from-literal=token=$GITHUB_PAT

# 4. Apply the Pipeline
kubectl apply -f examples/quickstart/pipeline.yaml

# 5. Apply org PolicyGates
kubectl apply -f examples/quickstart/policy-gates.yaml

# 6. Create a Bundle
kardinal create bundle kardinal-test-app \
  --image ghcr.io/pnz1990/kardinal-test-app:sha-9349a3f

# 7. Watch promotion start
kardinal get pipelines
# PIPELINE              BUNDLE        TEST       UAT     PROD     AGE
# kardinal-test-app     sha-9349a3f   Verified   ...     ...      2m

# 8. Check policy gate explanation
kardinal explain kardinal-test-app --env prod
# Must show: PolicyGates evaluated, reason why prod is waiting or ready

# 9. Prod PR is opened automatically
# Must open a PR titled: "[kardinal] Promote kardinal-test-app sha-9349a3f to prod"
# PR body must contain: artifact info, upstream verification, policy compliance table

# 10. After PR merge
kardinal get pipelines
# PIPELINE              BUNDLE        TEST       UAT        PROD       AGE
# kardinal-test-app     sha-9349a3f   Verified   Verified   Verified   8m

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] kardinal version returns valid version strings
  • [ ] kubectl apply -f examples/quickstart/pipeline.yaml succeeds with no errors
  • [ ] Bundle creation triggers automatic promotion to test and uat
  • [ ] PolicyGates block prod on weekends (verify with kardinal explain)
  • [ ] kardinal explain kardinal-test-app --env prod shows gate evaluation with values
  • [ ] A PR is opened for prod with structured evidence body (not a raw diff)
  • [ ] After PR merge, kardinal get pipelines shows PROD=Verified
  • [ ] kardinal get steps kardinal-test-app shows all steps with correct states

docs.title: Journey 2: Multi-Cluster Fleet — Parallel Prod with Argo Rollouts
docs.text:
Source: examples/multi-cluster-fleet/, AWS workshops: - https://catalog.workshops.aws/platform-engineering-on-eks/en-US/30-progressiveapplicationdelivery/40-production-deploy-kargo - https://github.com/aws-samples/fleet-management-on-amazon-eks-workshop/tree/mainline/patterns/kro-eks-cluster-mgmt#promote-the-application-to-prod-clusters

The user story: A platform engineer promotes rollouts-demo through test → pre-prod → [prod-eu, prod-us] in parallel. Prod environments use Argo Rollouts canary. Argo CD hub-spoke manages 4 clusters. Two PRs are opened in parallel for prod-eu and prod-us.

docs.title: Exact steps that must work
docs.text:
# 1. Apply the multi-cluster pipeline
kubectl apply -f examples/multi-cluster-fleet/pipeline.yaml

# 2. Apply org PolicyGates
kubectl apply -f examples/multi-cluster-fleet/policy-gates.yaml

# 3. Create an Argo CD ApplicationSet for all 4 clusters
kubectl apply -f examples/multi-cluster-fleet/argocd-applications.yaml

# 4. Create a Bundle
kardinal create bundle rollouts-demo \
  --image ghcr.io/myorg/rollouts-demo:v2.0.0

# 5. Watch the DAG-structured pipeline
kardinal get pipelines
# PIPELINE        BUNDLE   TEST       PRE-PROD   PROD-EU   PROD-US   AGE
# rollouts-demo   v2.0.0   Verified   Verified   PR open   PR open   15m

# 6. Both prod PRs are opened simultaneously (parallel fan-out)
# Two PRs must exist concurrently, both labeled kardinal

# 7. After merging both PRs, Argo Rollouts canary runs in each cluster
kardinal get steps rollouts-demo
# prod-eu: HealthChecking (delegated to argoRollouts)
# prod-us: HealthChecking (delegated to argoRollouts)

# 8. When rollouts complete
kardinal get pipelines
# PROD-EU: Verified, PROD-US: Verified

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] dependsOn fan-out works: prod-eu and prod-us start simultaneously after pre-prod
  • [ ] Two PRs opened in parallel, both with evidence and policy compliance
  • [ ] kardinal explain rollouts-demo --env prod-eu shows gate states correctly
  • [ ] Health adapter reads Argo CD Application status from hub cluster
  • [ ] Argo Rollouts delegation: health type argoRollouts waits for Rollout.status.phase=Healthy
  • [ ] Both prod regions reach Verified independently

docs.title: Journey 3: Policy Governance
docs.text:
Source: docs/policy-gates.md

The user story: A platform engineer adds a no-weekend-deploys PolicyGate and an upstream soak-time gate, verifies they block prod promotion, and uses kardinal policy simulate to preview the result. A second engineer adds a team-level gate without touching org gates.

docs.title: Exact steps that must work
docs.text:
# 1. Apply org gates (time-based and soak-time)
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kardinal.io/v1alpha1
kind: PolicyGate
metadata:
  name: no-weekend-deploys
  namespace: platform-policies
  labels:
    kardinal.io/scope: org
    kardinal.io/applies-to: prod
spec:
  expression: "!schedule.isWeekend"
  message: "Production deployments are blocked on weekends"
  recheckInterval: 5m
---
apiVersion: kardinal.io/v1alpha1
kind: PolicyGate
metadata:
  name: staging-soak-30m
  namespace: platform-policies
  labels:
    kardinal.io/scope: org
    kardinal.io/applies-to: prod
spec:
  expression: "bundle.upstreamSoakMinutes >= 30"
  message: "Must soak in staging for 30 minutes before promoting to prod"
  recheckInterval: 2m
EOF

# 2. Verify they're listed
kardinal policy list
# Must show:
#   no-weekend-deploys  [org] applies-to: prod  recheckInterval: 5m
#   staging-soak-30m    [org] applies-to: prod  recheckInterval: 2m

# 3. Simulate a weekend promotion
kardinal policy simulate --pipeline kardinal-test-app --env prod --time "Saturday 3pm"
# RESULT: BLOCKED
# Blocked by: no-weekend-deploys
# Message: "Production deployments are blocked on weekends"
# Next window: Monday 00:00 UTC

# 4. Simulate with soak-time insufficient
kardinal policy simulate --pipeline kardinal-test-app --env prod \
  --time "Tuesday 10am" --soak-minutes 10
# RESULT: BLOCKED
# Blocked by: staging-soak-30m
# bundle.upstreamSoakMinutes = 10 (threshold: >= 30)
# ETA: ~20 minutes

# 5. Simulate both gates passing
kardinal policy simulate --pipeline kardinal-test-app --env prod \
  --time "Tuesday 10am" --soak-minutes 45
# RESULT: PASS
# no-weekend-deploys: PASS (Tuesday 10:00 UTC, isWeekend=false)
# staging-soak-30m:   PASS (soakMinutes=45 >= 30)

# 6. Apply a team-level gate in a different namespace
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kardinal.io/v1alpha1
kind: PolicyGate
metadata:
  name: no-bot-deploys
  namespace: my-team
  labels:
    kardinal.io/scope: team
    kardinal.io/applies-to: prod
spec:
  expression: 'bundle.provenance.author != "dependabot[bot]"'
  message: "Automated dependency updates must be manually promoted to prod"
  recheckInterval: 5m
EOF

# 7. Verify both org and team gates appear
kardinal policy list
# Must show no-weekend-deploys [org], staging-soak-30m [org], no-bot-deploys [team]

# 8. Verify gates in Graph
kardinal explain kardinal-test-app --env prod
# Must show all three gates as nodes with current evaluation state

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Both org-level PolicyGates (time-based and soak-time) apply correctly
  • [ ] kardinal policy list shows gates with scope, applies-to, and recheckInterval
  • [ ] kardinal policy simulate --time "Saturday 3pm" returns BLOCKED with reason
  • [ ] kardinal policy simulate --soak-minutes 10 returns BLOCKED on soak gate
  • [ ] kardinal policy simulate with both gates passing returns PASS with table
  • [ ] Team-level gate is additive alongside org gates
  • [ ] Team cannot delete or modify org gates in platform-policies namespace (RBAC verified)
  • [ ] kardinal explain shows all three gates as nodes with CEL expression and current value
  • [ ] Gates appear as nodes in the promotion Graph (visible in kardinal get steps)
  • [ ] Soak gate re-evaluates after recheckInterval without manual trigger

docs.title: Journey 4: Rollback
docs.text:
Source: docs/rollback.md

The user story: A bundle is promoted to prod. The engineer discovers a bug and rolls back. One command. One PR. Same policy gates. Same audit trail.

docs.title: Exact steps that must work
docs.text:
# Assume kardinal-test-app v1.29.0 is verified in prod

# 1. Promote a bad version
kardinal create bundle kardinal-test-app --image ghcr.io/pnz1990/kardinal-test-app:sha-badbad0
# (goes through pipeline, reaches prod)

# 2. Roll back
kardinal rollback kardinal-test-app --env prod
# Rolling back kardinal-test-app in prod: v1.30.0-bad -> v1.29.0
# PR #N opened: https://github.com/.../pull/N

# 3. PR has kardinal/rollback label, same evidence structure as a forward promotion
# Must show previous version info, not just a diff

# 4. After merge
kardinal get pipelines
# PROD: v1.29.0 Verified

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] kardinal rollback opens a PR with kardinal/rollback label
  • [ ] Rollback PR has the same evidence structure as a promotion PR
  • [ ] After merge, the environment reflects the rolled-back version
  • [ ] kardinal history kardinal-test-app shows both the promotion and the rollback

docs.title: Journey 5: CLI — Core Operator Workflow
docs.text:
Source: docs/cli-reference.md

Every CLI command documented in docs/cli-reference.md must produce output matching the documented format.

docs.title: Commands that must work
docs.text:
kardinal version                          # CLI + controller versions
kardinal get pipelines                    # table with PIPELINE/BUNDLE/ENV columns
kardinal get steps <pipeline>             # PromotionSteps + PolicyGates with states
kardinal get bundles <pipeline>           # Bundle history with provenance
kardinal create bundle <pipeline> --image # creates Bundle CRD, prints confirmation
kardinal promote <pipeline> --env <env>   # triggers promotion, prints PR URL
kardinal explain <pipeline> --env <env>   # policy gate trace with current values
kardinal rollback <pipeline> --env <env>  # opens rollback PR
kardinal pause <pipeline>                 # injects freeze gate
kardinal resume <pipeline>                # removes freeze gate
kardinal history <pipeline>               # promotion history with evidence
kardinal policy list                      # all PolicyGates with scope
kardinal policy simulate                  # gate simulation with result

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Every command above executes without error
  • [ ] Output format matches examples in docs/cli-reference.md
  • [ ] kardinal explain includes CEL expression, current value, and result
  • [ ] kardinal policy simulate accepts --time flag and returns correct block/pass

docs.title: Journey 6: Rendered Manifests — Pre-Rendered GitOps
docs.text:
Source: docs/rendered-manifests.md, examples/rendered-manifests/

The user story: A platform engineer configures a pipeline that renders Kustomize manifests at promotion time and commits the raw YAML output to environment-specific branches. Argo CD syncs from the rendered branches. PR reviewers see exact YAML diffs — no template expansion required. The GitOps agent never runs kustomize build.

This pattern is the enterprise standard for large Argo CD deployments (reduces agent CPU load, enables CODEOWNERS on rendered output, surfaces hidden config changes in PRs).

docs.title: Exact steps that must work
docs.text:
# 1. GitOps repo has a "DRY" source branch and rendered environment branches
# Structure:
#   source/      (DRY: Kustomize base + overlays)
#   env/dev      (rendered: plain YAML for dev)
#   env/staging  (rendered: plain YAML for staging)
#   env/prod     (rendered: plain YAML for prod)

# 2. Apply the Pipeline with branch layout and kustomize-build step
kubectl apply -f examples/rendered-manifests/pipeline.yaml

# Pipeline uses layout: branch with kustomize-build in the step sequence:
# steps:
#   - uses: git-clone         # checks out source branch
#   - uses: kustomize-set-image
#   - uses: kustomize-build   # renders manifests to stdout
#   - uses: git-commit        # commits rendered YAML to env/prod branch
#   - uses: open-pr           # PR: env/prod-incoming -> env/prod
#   - uses: wait-for-merge
#   - uses: health-check

# 3. Create a Bundle
kardinal create bundle rendered-demo \
  --image ghcr.io/myorg/rendered-demo:v2.0.0

# 4. Inspect the PR
# PR must contain a rendered YAML diff (actual line-by-line YAML changes)
# not a diff of the kustomization.yaml values file
kardinal get steps rendered-demo
# prod: WaitingForMerge PR #N (rendered branch diff visible in GitHub)

# 5. After merge
kardinal get pipelines
# rendered-demo: PROD=Verified

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] layout: branch with kustomize-build step renders manifests and commits to env branch
  • [ ] PR diff shows rendered YAML, not template source
  • [ ] Argo CD Application tracking env/prod branch reflects the merged content
  • [ ] kardinal explain shows the branch each environment tracks
  • [ ] Source branch (source/) is never modified by the promotion (only env branches change)
  • [ ] Bundle supersession during an in-flight render: old render is discarded, new render begins

docs.title: Journey 7: Multi-Tenant Self-Service — Team Onboarding via ApplicationSet
docs.text:
Source: docs/advanced-patterns.md, examples/multi-tenant/

The user story: A platform team uses Argo CD ApplicationSets to provision promotion pipelines automatically when a developer creates a new service folder in a central repository. kardinal-promoter Pipelines are generated alongside the Argo CD Applications. A new team member commits a folder to Git and receives a complete 3-environment promotion pipeline without any manual platform team intervention.

This is the "nested ApplicationSet" pattern described in Kargo and Akuity workshops as the target state for large-scale platform engineering.

docs.title: Exact steps that must work
docs.text:
# 1. Platform team installs the root ApplicationSet
kubectl apply -f examples/multi-tenant/root-appset.yaml

# root-appset.yaml watches the teams/ directory in the platform repo.
# When a new folder appears, it creates:
#   - A Namespace for the team
#   - An Argo CD Application for each environment
#   - A kardinal Pipeline for the team's service

# 2. Developer creates a new service
mkdir teams/payment-service
cat > teams/payment-service/pipeline-values.yaml <<EOF
image: ghcr.io/myorg/payment-service
environments: [dev, staging, prod]
prodApproval: pr-review
EOF
git add . && git commit -m "feat: add payment-service" && git push

# 3. ApplicationSet detects the new folder and provisions the Pipeline
kubectl get pipeline -n payment-service
# NAME              ENVS   STATUS
# payment-service   3      Ready

# 4. Team creates their first Bundle from CI
kardinal create bundle payment-service \
  --namespace payment-service \
  --image ghcr.io/myorg/payment-service:v1.0.0

# 5. Verify isolation: pipeline only affects payment-service namespace
kardinal get pipelines --all-namespaces
# NAMESPACE          PIPELINE          BUNDLE   STATUS
# payment-service    payment-service   v1.0.0   Promoting
# checkout-service   checkout-service  v3.1.2   Verified

docs.title: Pass criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] ApplicationSet creates a Pipeline CRD when a new team folder is committed to Git
  • [ ] Pipeline is scoped to the team's namespace; org PolicyGates are inherited automatically
  • [ ] Team cannot see or modify another team's Pipeline (RBAC isolation)
  • [ ] kardinal get pipelines --namespace payment-service shows only that team's pipelines
  • [ ] Org-level PolicyGate in platform-policies blocks prod promotion for the new team's pipeline on weekends
  • [ ] Deleting the team folder from Git triggers ApplicationSet deletion of the Pipeline CRD (cascade)

docs.title: Journey Status
docs.text:
Updated by the coordinator after each batch.

Rule (Issue #418): A journey is only marked ✅ when: 1. Its TestJourneyN test passes in CI on main branch (automated evidence), OR 2. A [PDCA AUTOMATED] comment on Issue #1 confirms live cluster validation with real images 3. Every code example in the relevant doc page runs without error

Manual coordinator comments alone do NOT count as evidence.

docs.title: How Journeys Map to Roadmap Stages
docs.text: Stage Journeys it enables 0: Project Skeleton All (prerequisites) 1: CRD Types All (type system) 2: Bundle + Pipeline Reconcilers All (basic flow) 3: Graph Generation All (core engine) 4: PolicyGate CEL Evaluator J1 partial, J3 full, J7 partial 5: Git Operations + GitHub PR J1 full, J4 full, J6 partial 6: PromotionStep Reconciler J1 full end-to-end, J6 full 7: Health Adapters J1 with Argo CD, J2 partial 8: CLI J5 full 9: kardinal-ui UI visibility (bonus) 10: PR Evidence + Webhook J1 PR quality, J4 rollback 11: GitHub Action + kardinal init J1 CI integration 13: Rollback J4 full 14: Distributed Mode J2 full with agents 15: MetricCheck J3 metrics gates

docs.title: The Acceptance Test Suite
docs.text:
When a journey is marked ✅, it means the following passes in CI on a kind cluster:

make test-e2e-journey-1    # quickstart end-to-end
make test-e2e-journey-2    # multi-cluster fleet (simulated)
make test-e2e-journey-3    # policy governance (time gate + soak gate + team gate)
make test-e2e-journey-4    # rollback
make test-e2e-journey-5    # CLI commands
make test-e2e-journey-6    # rendered manifests (branch layout + kustomize-build)
make test-e2e-journey-7    # multi-tenant self-service (ApplicationSet + Pipeline provisioning)

These E2E tests are the final arbiter of project completeness. Unit tests are necessary but not sufficient. A feature is done when its journey test passes.

docs.text:

  • Spec: .specify/specs/<feature>/spec.md
  • Design doc: docs/design/<feature>.md

docs.text:

  • [ ] Given [...], When [...], Then [...]

docs.text: # Verify all [X] tasks have real implementation (paste output)

docs.text:

Behavior matches docs/: - [ ] docs/quickstart.md step X works as documented - [ ] docs/concepts.md behavior X is correct

docs.title: Pre-merge Checklist (engineer completes before requesting review)
docs.text:
• [ ] go test ./... -race passes
  • [ ] go vet ./... zero findings
  • [ ] All [X] tasks have real implementation (no phantom completions)
  • [ ] All acceptance criteria from spec.md implemented
  • [ ] Manual kubectl validation output included above
  • [ ] All new .go files have Apache 2.0 copyright header
  • [ ] No util.go, helpers.go, common.go created
  • [ ] No new entry in go.mod require block (or needs-human label set)
  • [ ] Every new reconciler has an idempotency test
  • [ ] No kro module import

docs.title: kardinal-promoter: Development Roadmap
docs.text: Created: 2026-04-09 Status: Active Based on: docs/aide/vision.md

docs.title: ⚠️ Current Gate: Workshop 1 Execution (epic #123)
docs.text: The coordinator must not generate queues for Workshop 2 scope until epic #123 (milestone workshop-1-executed) is closed.

docs.title: Phase A — Fix the three code gaps (assign immediately):
docs.text:
1. Issue #115 — kardinal get pipelines per-env columns
  2. Issue #116 — kardinal explain label mismatch
  3. Issue #117 — kardinal explain CEL expression display

docs.title: Phase B — Complete kind cluster E2E (item 026, in progress)
Phase C — Execute the workshop (coordinator assigns this as an item):
docs.text: The standalone agent runs every step in epic #123 on a live kind cluster. Records terminal output. Posts [WORKSHOP 1 EXECUTED] on Issue #1. Closes epic #123. Closes milestone workshop-1-executed.

docs.title: Phase D — Workshop 2 scope begins:
docs.text: Only after #123 is closed. Coordinator may then generate queues for:

docs.title: Stage 0: Project Skeleton
docs.text: Goal: Establish the Go module, directory layout, build tooling, and CI scaffolding so that every subsequent stage has a working build baseline and nowhere to step on each other.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Go module github.com/kardinal-promoter/kardinal-promoter with go.mod and go.sum
  • Canonical directory layout (matches AGENTS.md Package Layout and all design specs): 
    cmd/kardinal-controller/   # controller binary entry point
cmd/kardinal/              # CLI binary entry point
pkg/
  graph/                   # Graph CRD client + builder (spec 001)
  translator/              # Pipeline → Graph translation (spec 002)
  reconciler/
    promotionstep/         # state machine + evidence (spec 003)
    policygate/            # CEL evaluation + timer recheck (spec 004)
  health/                  # health adapter interface + implementations (spec 005)
  steps/                   # step engine + built-in steps (spec 008)
  scm/                     # GitHub SCM provider
  update/                  # kustomize/helm update strategies
  cel/                     # shared CEL environment
web/
  embed.go                 # go:embed all:dist (spec 006)
  src/                     # React 19 UI
config/crd/                # CRD YAML manifests (generated by controller-gen)
config/rbac/               # ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding manifests
config/manager/            # controller Deployment manifests
examples/quickstart/       # quickstart example manifests
examples/multi-cluster-fleet/
  • Makefile with targets: generate, manifests, build, test, lint, docker-build, install (applies CRDs to current cluster)
  • kubebuilder scaffolding for three CRDs: Pipeline, Bundle, PolicyGate
  • controller-gen generating DeepCopy, CRD YAML, and RBAC markers
  • GitHub Actions CI workflow: go build ./..., go vet ./..., golangci-lint, go test ./...
  • .golangci.yml with baseline linter config
  • Dockerfile (multi-stage, distroless final image)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: None — this is the foundation.

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• make build produces bin/kardinal-controller and bin/kardinal on Linux amd64
  • make test runs go test ./... with zero failures
  • make manifests regenerates CRD YAML without diff (idempotent)
  • make docker-build produces a runnable container image
  • CI workflow passes on every push to main

docs.title: Stage 1: CRD Types and Validation
docs.text: Goal: Define the complete Go types and OpenAPI validation schemas for all user-facing CRDs. This stage produces no running controller — only the schema artifacts that every downstream stage depends on.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Pipeline CRD type with:
  • spec.environments[] (name, gitRepo, branch, path, approvalMode auto|pr-review, updateStrategy kustomize|helm, healthAdapter, deliveryDelegate, dependsOn)
  • spec.policyGates[] (reference by name/namespace)
  • status.conditions[], status.phase
  • Bundle CRD type with:
  • spec.type (image|config|mixed)
  • spec.images[] (repository, tag, digest)
  • spec.configRef (gitRepo, commitSHA)
  • spec.provenance (commitSHA, ciRunURL, author, timestamp)
  • spec.intent (targetEnvironment, skipEnvironments[])
  • status.phase (Available|Promoting|Verified|Failed|Superseded)
  • status.environments[] per-environment evidence (gateResults, approver, mergedAt, healthCheckedAt)
  • PolicyGate CRD type with:
  • spec.expression (CEL expression string)
  • spec.message (human-readable description shown when gate blocks)
  • spec.recheckInterval
  • spec.skipPermission (bool)
  • spec.selector (environment label selector for org-level auto-injection)
  • status.lastEvaluatedAt, status.ready (bool), status.reason
  • PromotionStep CRD type (controller-internal, not user-created) with per-step state and output accumulator
  • All types have +kubebuilder:validation markers for required fields, enums, and formats
  • Generated CRD YAML passes kubectl apply --dry-run=server against a kind cluster with no errors
  • Kubebuilder sample manifests in config/samples/ for each CRD

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 0

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• kubectl apply -f config/crd/bases/ against a fresh kind cluster installs all CRDs without error
  • kubectl apply -f config/samples/ creates valid objects that pass server-side validation
  • go vet ./... and golangci-lint pass with no new warnings
  • Roundtrip JSON marshaling test passes for each CRD type

docs.title: Stage 2: Bundle and Pipeline Reconcilers (No-Op Baseline)
docs.text: Goal: Implement the controller binary with two reconcilers that watch Bundle and Pipeline objects and emit structured log events, without yet driving any real promotion logic. Establishes the reconciler loop, status patching, and leader election.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• cmd/kardinal-controller/main.go wiring controller-runtime Manager with:
  • Leader election via Kubernetes Lease
  • Health/readiness probes on :8081
  • Metrics on :8080
  • Configurable log level via --zap-log-level flag
  • BundleReconciler that:
  • Watches Bundle objects
  • Sets status.phase = Available on newly created Bundles
  • Emits a structured log event with bundle name, type, and target environment
  • Patches status via status subresource with optimistic locking
  • PipelineReconciler that:
  • Watches Pipeline objects
  • Sets status.conditions with type: Ready, status: False, reason: Initializing on new Pipelines
  • Validates that environments have unique names and that dependsOn references are valid
  • Emits structured log event with environment count
  • Helm chart in chart/kardinal-promoter/ with:
  • controller Deployment, ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, Service
  • values.yaml with image tag, replica count, log level
  • make install deploys the chart to the current cluster
  • Integration test: apply a Bundle and Pipeline CRD to a kind cluster, verify status.phase = Available and status.conditions[0].type = Ready within 10 seconds

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 1

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Controller starts and passes liveness/readiness probes within 5 seconds
  • kubectl apply -f examples/quickstart/pipeline.yaml followed by kubectl apply -f examples/quickstart/bundle.yaml results in both objects having expected status within 10 seconds
  • Controller survives a pod restart (leader election re-acquired in under 15 seconds)
  • Prometheus /metrics endpoint exposes controller_runtime_reconcile_total for both reconcilers
  • No goroutine leaks under 100 rapid creates/deletes (verified by go test -race)

docs.title: Stage 3: Graph Generation and kro Integration
docs.text: Goal: Implement the Pipeline-to-Graph translator. For a given Bundle+Pipeline pair, the controller generates a kro Graph spec with correctly ordered PromotionStep and PolicyGate nodes, with dependency edges derived from CEL references.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• pkg/graph package with:
  • Builder struct: takes a Pipeline and a list of applicable PolicyGate objects, returns a kro.run/v1alpha1/Graph spec
  • Node generation: one PromotionStep node per environment, one PolicyGate node per gate per environment
  • Sequential default edges (env[0] → env[1] → ...) and parallel edges from dependsOn
  • Org-level gate injection: gates in platform-policies namespace with matching selector are prepended to each environment's gate list; teams cannot remove them
  • ownerReferences on the Graph pointing to the Bundle (cascade delete on Bundle GC)
  • BundleReconciler extended to:
  • On status.phase = Available, call graph.Builder and create the Graph object if not already present
  • Set status.phase = Promoting and record graph name in status
  • Watch for Graph status changes and reflect them in Bundle status
  • Unit tests for graph.Builder covering: single-env linear pipeline, 3-env linear pipeline, parallel fan-out with dependsOn, org-level gate injection, missing gate reference (error)
  • examples/quickstart/graph-expected.yaml showing the expected generated Graph for the quickstart Pipeline

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 2
  • kro Graph controller CRD installed in cluster (tested against kro experimental branch)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• For the quickstart example (3-env linear pipeline with 1 PolicyGate), the generated Graph has exactly 3 PromotionStep nodes and 1 PolicyGate node with correct dependency edges
  • kubectl get graph -n <bundle-namespace> shows the generated Graph within 5 seconds of Bundle creation
  • Deleting the Bundle cascades to Graph deletion within 30 seconds
  • Unit tests for graph.Builder achieve 90% line coverage
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 4: PolicyGate CEL Evaluator
docs.text: Goal: Implement the PolicyGateReconciler that evaluates CEL expressions against a populated context. Gates block or allow promotion advancement at their position in the Graph.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• pkg/cel package with:
  • Evaluator struct wrapping google/cel-go
  • BundleContext struct providing all CEL variables: bundle.type, bundle.images, bundle.provenance, bundle.intent, schedule.isWeekend, schedule.hour, schedule.dayOfWeek, environment.name
  • Evaluate(expr string, ctx BundleContext) (bool, string, error) — returns pass/fail, reason string, and error
  • Benchmark: Evaluate completes in under 10ms for a 10-clause expression
  • PolicyGateReconciler that:
  • Watches PolicyGate objects referenced in active Graphs
  • On each reconcile, builds BundleContext from the owning Bundle's status
  • Calls Evaluator.Evaluate and patches status.ready (bool) and status.lastEvaluatedAt
  • Requeues after spec.recheckInterval for time-based gates
  • Marks the associated Graph node as passed or blocked
  • Built-in gate templates in config/samples/gates/:
  • no-weekend-deploys.yaml: blocks when schedule.isWeekend == true
  • business-hours-only.yaml: blocks when schedule.hour < 9 || schedule.hour > 17
  • upstream-soak-required.yaml: blocks until bundle.status.environments[upstream].healthCheckedAt is older than 30 minutes (Phase 2 prerequisite structure)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 3

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Apply no-weekend-deploys.yaml gate and a Bundle on a Saturday: status.ready = false, status.reason contains "weekend"
  • Apply same gate on a Tuesday: status.ready = true within 5 seconds
  • Gate with recheckInterval: 1m re-evaluates without manual trigger
  • cel.Evaluate benchmark passes: p99 under 10ms on 1000 iterations
  • Org-level gate in platform-policies namespace cannot be removed by patching a Pipeline in another namespace (RBAC test)
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 5: Git Operations and GitHub PR Flow
docs.text: Goal: Implement the Git clone/push and GitHub PR lifecycle. The controller can open a PR with a manifest update, detect merge via webhook, and mark the environment as merged.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• pkg/scm package with:
  • SCMProvider interface: OpenPR, ClosePR, CommentOnPR, GetPRStatus, ParseWebhookEvent
  • GitHubProvider implementation using google/go-github
  • GitClient interface: Clone, Checkout, CommitAll, Push
  • GoGitClient implementation using go-git/go-git
  • Shallow clone with --depth=1 and shared work trees under a temp directory (target: clone from cache in under 2 seconds)
  • Built-in promotion steps in pkg/steps/:
  • git-clone: clones the target repo at the environment path
  • kustomize-set-image: runs kustomize edit set image for each image in the Bundle
  • kustomize-build: runs kustomize build and validates output
  • git-commit: stages changes and creates a commit with structured message including Bundle name and provenance
  • git-push: pushes the branch to the remote
  • open-pr: calls SCMProvider.OpenPR with PR body template (see F6)
  • wait-for-merge: polls SCMProvider.GetPRStatus; resolves on merge
  • PR body template in pkg/scm/pr_template.go:
  • Policy gate compliance table (gate name | result | reason)
  • Artifact provenance table (image | tag | digest | CI run link | commit SHA)
  • Upstream verification table (environment | health checked at | elapsed)
  • Webhook endpoint at /webhook/scm in the controller HTTP server:
  • Validates X-Hub-Signature-256
  • On pull_request event with action: closed, merged: true, reconciles the owning Bundle
  • Startup reconciliation: on controller start, list all in-flight PRs and re-check merge status
  • GITHUB_TOKEN sourced from a Kubernetes Secret referenced in Pipeline spec.gitCredentials

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 4

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• E2E test (kind cluster + real GitHub repo or ghcli mock):
  • Apply a Pipeline and Bundle
  • Controller opens a PR on the target repo within 10 seconds
  • PR body contains at least the provenance table and gate compliance table
  • Merge the PR (via GitHub API in test)
  • Controller detects merge within 15 seconds and advances Bundle to next environment
  • git-clone step completes in under 2 seconds for a repo cached in the work directory
  • PR creation completes in under 5 seconds (mocked GitHub API)
  • make test passes with all new unit tests

docs.title: Stage 6: PromotionStep Reconciler and Full Promotion Loop
docs.text: Goal: Wire the full promotion loop end-to-end. A Bundle created against a 3-environment Pipeline with a PolicyGate flows from Available through Promoting to Verified, opening PRs, running health checks (stubbed), and evaluating gates at each step.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• PromotionStepReconciler that:
  • Watches PromotionStep objects created by kro Graph
  • Reads the step type from the Graph node spec and dispatches to the corresponding steps.Step implementation
  • Passes outputs between steps via an accumulator stored in PromotionStep.status.outputs
  • Sets PromotionStep.status.phase to Running | Succeeded | Failed | Blocked
  • On Failed, sets the owning Bundle's phase to Failed with reason
  • Default step sequence inferred from Pipeline environment config:
  • approvalMode: pr-review → [git-clone, kustomize-set-image, kustomize-build, git-commit, git-push, open-pr, wait-for-merge, health-check]
  • approvalMode: auto → [git-clone, kustomize-set-image, kustomize-build, git-commit, git-push, health-check]
  • health-check step stubbed to succeed immediately in Stage 6 (real adapters in Stage 7)
  • Bundle phase machine: Available → Promoting → Verified | Failed | Superseded
  • Bundle supersession: when a new Bundle targeting the same Pipeline is created, older Bundles in Promoting are set to Superseded and their Graphs are deleted
  • kardinal explain CLI command reading live PromotionStep and PolicyGate statuses: 
    Environment  Step           Status    Reason
test         kustomize-set-image  Succeeded
test         open-pr        Succeeded  PR #42
test         wait-for-merge Succeeded  merged by alice
uat          open-pr        Running    PR #43 open
prod         PolicyGate     Blocked    no-weekend-deploys: weekend
  • Quickstart example updated: examples/quickstart/ end-to-end walkthrough produces a working promotion

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 5

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• E2E test: kind cluster + GitHub repo + 3-environment Pipeline + no-weekend-deploys gate
  • Create Bundle on a weekday
  • Bundle reaches Verified with all environments promoted within 5 minutes (using auto-merge in test)
  • Create Bundle on a weekend (or with mocked schedule)
  • Bundle stops at prod gate with status.phase = Promoting, prod PromotionStep Blocked
  • kardinal explain output matches expected table
  • Bundle supersession: creating a second Bundle while first is promoting sets first to Superseded
  • All reconcilers are idempotent: deleting and re-creating a PromotionStep does not duplicate PRs
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 7: Health Adapters
docs.text: Goal: Replace the health-check stub with real health verification. The controller checks Deployment readiness, Argo CD Application health+sync, or Flux Kustomization Ready — whichever is present.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• pkg/health package with:
  • Adapter interface: Check(ctx, env Environment) (HealthResult, error) where HealthResult carries phase, message, and checked-at timestamp
  • DeploymentAdapter: watches apps/v1/Deployment readiness conditions in the target namespace
  • ArgoCDAdapter: watches argoproj.io/v1alpha1/Application health (Healthy) and sync (Synced) status
  • FluxAdapter: watches kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1/Kustomization Ready condition
  • AutoDetector: on controller start and every 5 minutes, lists installed CRDs; selects adapter by priority: argocd > flux > resource
  • Remote cluster support: adapter can be configured with a kubeconfig Secret reference; uses rest.Config from the Secret data
  • health-check step: calls the auto-detected adapter; polls every 10 seconds until Healthy or until timeout (default 30 minutes, configurable via Pipeline spec.environments[].healthTimeout)
  • Health result written to Bundle.status.environments[].healthCheckedAt for upstream soak time calculation in PolicyGates
  • examples/quickstart/ updated with Argo CD Application resource and health adapter config

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 6

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Unit test: DeploymentAdapter returns Healthy when all pods are ready, Degraded otherwise
  • Integration test (kind + Argo CD installed):
  • Pipeline uses healthAdapter: argocd
  • After PR merge, controller waits for Argo CD Application to become Healthy+Synced before advancing
  • Integration test (kind, no Argo CD):
  • AutoDetector falls back to DeploymentAdapter
  • Bundle advances after Deployment becomes ready
  • Bundle.status.environments[test].healthCheckedAt is set after health check passes
  • Remote kubeconfig Secret test: adapter connects to a second kind cluster and reads Deployment health
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 8: CLI
docs.text: Goal: Deliver the kardinal CLI binary with the full command set defined in F9. All commands read/write CRDs via client-go using the current kubeconfig context.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• cmd/kardinal/main.go with cobra root command and subcommands:
  • kardinal init — interactive wizard generating a Pipeline YAML from 8 lines of config (envs, repo, update strategy)
  • kardinal get pipelines [name] — table output with phase and environment count
  • kardinal get bundles [name] — table with phase, type, and target env
  • kardinal get steps [bundle] — table matching explain output
  • kardinal create bundle <pipeline> --image repo:tag [--type image|config] — creates a Bundle CRD (pipeline is a positional arg; use --image flag for image reference)
  • kardinal promote <pipeline> --env <environment> — manually trigger promotion to a specific environment
  • kardinal explain <pipeline> --env <environment> [--watch] — rich DAG table with gate trace; --watch streams updates
  • kardinal rollback <pipeline> --env <environment> [--to version] [--emergency] — creates a rollback Bundle; --emergency sets a label that bypasses skipPermission gates (RBAC-protected)
  • kardinal pause <pipeline> — injects a PolicyGate with expression: "false" to freeze all in-flight promotions
  • kardinal resume <pipeline> — deletes the freeze PolicyGate
  • kardinal history <pipeline> — lists Bundle history with phases and timings
  • kardinal policy list — lists PolicyGates with current result and last evaluated
  • kardinal policy test <file> — validate CEL syntax, verify context attributes, dry-run against sample context
  • kardinal policy simulate --pipeline <name> --env <environment> [--time "..."] [--soak-minutes N] — runs all gates for a hypothetical context, prints pass/fail table
  • kardinal diff <bundle-a> <bundle-b> — shows the artifact differences between two Bundles (image tags, digests, commits)
  • kardinal version — prints CLI and controller versions
  • Shell completion scripts (bash, zsh, fish) via cobra.GenBashCompletion
  • docs/cli-reference.md updated to match all new commands

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 6 (for CRD types and status shapes)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• kardinal init generates a valid Pipeline YAML that passes kubectl apply --dry-run=server
  • kardinal create bundle ... creates a Bundle; kardinal get bundles shows it within 2 seconds
  • kardinal explain --watch streams updates without terminal flicker (uses ANSI escape codes for in-place refresh)
  • kardinal policy test prints correct pass/fail for no-weekend-deploys gate on both weekday and weekend mock contexts
  • kardinal rollback requires the caller to have create on bundles in the target namespace (RBAC test)
  • All CLI commands tested with cobra/pflag unit tests and table-driven command output tests
  • make build produces a single static binary under 50 MB

docs.title: Stage 9: Embedded React UI
docs.text: Goal: Deliver kardinal-ui — a read-only React app embedded in the controller binary that renders the promotion DAG with per-node state.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• ui/ directory with Vite + React 19 + TypeScript project
  • DAG visualization using @dagrejs/dagre for layout and reactflow for rendering:
  • PromotionStep nodes colored green (Succeeded), amber (Running), red (Failed)
  • PolicyGate nodes colored green (Pass), red (Fail), grey (Pending)
  • Edges showing dependency direction
  • Clicking a node opens a detail panel with: step outputs, gate expression + reason, PR link, provenance
  • Pipeline list sidebar: clicking a Pipeline shows its active Bundle's DAG
  • Bundle history list per Pipeline with phase badges
  • Backend API in the controller at /api/v1/ui/:
  • GET /api/v1/ui/pipelines — list Pipelines
  • GET /api/v1/ui/pipelines/{name}/bundles — list Bundles for a Pipeline
  • GET /api/v1/ui/bundles/{name}/graph — return Graph nodes + edges + statuses
  • GET /api/v1/ui/bundles/{name}/steps — return PromotionStep list with statuses
  • GET /api/v1/ui/gates — return PolicyGate list with statuses
  • UI served at controller port 8082 via --ui-listen-address flag
  • ui/dist/ built by make ui and embedded in cmd/kardinal-controller/ via go:embed
  • make ui runs npm ci && npm run build in ui/
  • Separate UI port available (no auth in Phase 1; RBAC-proxy pattern documented)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 6 (for API shapes)
  • Stage 7 (for health status)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• make ui && make build produces a single controller binary that serves the UI
  • Navigating to http://localhost:8082 in a browser shows the Pipeline list
  • Clicking a Pipeline with an active Bundle shows a DAG with at least 3 nodes
  • A PolicyGate node in Fail state is red; clicking it shows the CEL expression and failure reason
  • PR link in node detail panel opens the correct GitHub URL
  • UI loads in under 2 seconds on localhost
  • No browser console errors on initial load

docs.title: Stage 10: PR Evidence, Labels, and Webhook Reliability
docs.text: Goal: Complete the PR evidence feature (F6) with the full structured body, GitHub labels, and a robust webhook + startup reconciliation path.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Full PR body template:
  • Policy gate compliance table: gate name | namespace | result | reason | last evaluated
  • Artifact provenance table: image | tag | digest | CI run | commit SHA | author
  • Upstream verification table: environment | health checked at | elapsed since check
  • Commit range diff link (GitHub compare URL between previous and new manifests SHA)
  • GitHub label management:
  • Ensure labels kardinal, kardinal/promotion, kardinal/rollback, kardinal/emergency exist in the repo on controller startup
  • Apply kardinal and kardinal/promotion to every promotion PR
  • Apply kardinal/rollback to rollback PRs; kardinal/emergency to emergency bypass PRs
  • Webhook improvements:
  • Retry failed webhook deliveries with exponential backoff (3 retries, max 30 seconds)
  • /webhook/scm/health endpoint returns 200 if webhook secret is configured
  • Metrics: kardinal_webhook_events_total{action, result} counter
  • Startup reconciliation:
  • On controller start, list all Bundles in Promoting phase; for each, call SCMProvider.GetPRStatus and re-sync state
  • Detects PRs merged during controller downtime

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 5
  • Stage 6

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• PR opened by controller contains all three tables with correct data
  • PR body is updated (via edit comment) when a gate re-evaluates from Fail to Pass
  • Labels are applied correctly on a real GitHub repo in E2E test
  • Restarting the controller while a PR is open: on restart, controller reconciles PR status within 30 seconds
  • kardinal_webhook_events_total{action="closed",result="merged"} counter increments on merge event
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 11: GitHub Actions Integration and kardinal init
docs.text: Goal: Deliver the GitHub Actions workflow that creates Bundles from CI and the kardinal init wizard that generates a working Pipeline from minimal input. Together, these complete the onboarding story for Phase 1b.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• GitHub Action kardinal-promoter/bundle-action at .github/actions/create-bundle/:
  • Inputs: pipeline, type (image|config), images (newline-separated repo:tag), namespace, kardinal-url
  • Authenticates via KARDINAL_TOKEN (bearer token sent to /api/v1/bundles endpoint on the controller)
  • Creates a Bundle by POST to /api/v1/bundles with provenance auto-populated from GITHUB_SHA, GITHUB_RUN_URL, GITHUB_ACTOR
  • Outputs: bundle-name, bundle-status-url
  • Bundle webhook endpoint at /api/v1/bundles:
  • Validates Bearer + HMAC signature
  • Creates a Bundle CRD and returns {name, namespace} JSON
  • Rate limited: 60 requests/minute per token
  • kardinal init wizard (extends Stage 8):
  • Prompts: application name, namespace, environments (comma-separated), Git repo URL, branch, path per env, update strategy, approval mode per env
  • Outputs a complete Pipeline YAML and a sample GitHub Actions workflow YAML
  • Validates that the generated YAML passes kubectl apply --dry-run=server before printing
  • Documentation: docs/ci-integration.md updated with Action usage, token setup, and example workflow

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 8 (CLI)
  • Stage 5 (webhook endpoint foundation)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions workflow using kardinal-promoter/bundle-action@v1 creates a Bundle visible via kardinal get bundles within 10 seconds of the workflow step completing
  • kardinal init produces a Pipeline YAML with correct environment ordering matching the prompts
  • Bundle webhook rejects requests without a valid HMAC signature (401)
  • Bundle webhook rejects more than 60 requests/minute from the same token (429)
  • docs/ci-integration.md example workflow passes yamllint

docs.title: Stage 12: Helm Strategy and Config-Only Promotions
docs.text: Goal: Add Helm as an update strategy and implement config-only Bundle promotion, completing the Phase 2 update strategy matrix and enabling promotion of configuration changes independent of image updates.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• helm-set-image step in pkg/steps/:
  • Updates image.tag in values.yaml or values-<env>.yaml using helm.sh/helm/v3 library (no shell exec)
  • Supports set paths specified in Pipeline spec.environments[].helm.imagePathTemplate
  • config-merge step in pkg/steps/:
  • Applies changes from Bundle.spec.configRef.commitSHA via cherry-pick or overlay strategy
  • Strategy configured per environment: cherry-pick (default) or overlay
  • Bundle.spec.type: config fully handled:
  • Step sequence for config bundles: [git-clone, config-merge, git-commit, git-push, open-pr, wait-for-merge, health-check]
  • Config Bundles do not supersede Image Bundles (separate supersession tracking per type)
  • examples/config-promotion/ with a complete config-only promotion example
  • Documentation: docs/concepts.md updated with config promotion section

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 6

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• E2E test: create a config Bundle referencing a commit SHA; controller opens a PR that applies the config change; merging advances the Bundle to Verified
  • Helm strategy test: Pipeline with updateStrategy: helm and helm.imagePathTemplate: .image.tag; controller updates values.yaml correctly
  • Config Bundle and Image Bundle for the same Pipeline can coexist and promote independently
  • make test passes with new step unit tests

docs.title: Stage 13: Rollback and Pause/Resume
docs.text: Goal: Implement kardinal rollback as a forward promotion of a prior Bundle and kardinal pause/resume with graceful gate behavior. Includes automatic rollback on consecutive health-check failures.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Rollback flow:
  • kardinal rollback <pipeline> --env <environment> [--to version] creates a new Bundle with spec.provenance.rollbackOf pointing to the original Bundle
  • Rollback Bundle has label kardinal.io/rollback: "true"; kardinal/rollback label applied to its PRs
  • Emergency rollback: --emergency flag sets kardinal.io/emergency: "true" label; PolicyGate evaluator skips gates where spec.skipPermission: true
  • RBAC: emergency rollback requires create verb on a rollbackemergency resource (custom verb enforced via webhook)
  • Automatic rollback:
  • Pipeline.spec.environments[].autoRollback.failureThreshold (default: 3 consecutive health-check failures)
  • Controller creates a rollback Bundle automatically; records reason in Bundle status
  • Pause/resume:
  • kardinal pause <pipeline> injects a PolicyGate with expression: "false" into the Pipeline's effective gate list; blocks all new promotion steps immediately
  • Existing PRs remain open; no new steps are started
  • kardinal resume <pipeline> deletes the freeze PolicyGate; reconciler re-queues all in-flight steps
  • kardinal rollback and kardinal pause documented in docs/rollback.md

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 6
  • Stage 8 (CLI)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• kardinal rollback my-app --env prod --to v1.2.3 creates a Bundle with rollbackOf in status; PR is labeled kardinal/rollback
  • Emergency rollback bypasses no-weekend-deploys gate (configured with skipPermission: true) on a mocked weekend
  • kardinal pause stops new steps from starting; existing open PRs are unaffected
  • Auto-rollback: inject 3 consecutive health-check failures in integration test; verify rollback Bundle is created automatically
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 14: Distributed Mode (Control Plane + Agents)
docs.text: Goal: Split the single binary into a control plane (Pipeline reconciliation, Graph generation, PolicyGate evaluation) and an agent (PromotionStep reconciliation per shard). Agents connect outbound to the control plane.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• cmd/kardinal-agent/ binary:
  • Connects to control plane via gRPC over mTLS (control plane address via --control-plane-address flag)
  • Watches PromotionStep objects labeled with kardinal.io/shard: <shard-id> only
  • Reconciles steps using the same steps.Step implementations as standalone mode
  • Git tokens and health credentials stay in the agent cluster (no forwarding to control plane)
  • Control plane changes:
  • kardinal-controller in distributed mode (--mode=control-plane) only reconciles Pipelines, Bundles, PolicyGates, and Graphs
  • Step dispatch: sets kardinal.io/shard label on PromotionStep objects based on Pipeline spec.shard field
  • Agent liveness: control plane tracks agent heartbeats; marks shard as unavailable if no heartbeat for 60 seconds
  • config/agent/ Helm chart for the agent binary
  • --mode=standalone (default) preserves Phase 1 behavior (single binary handles everything)
  • Agent reconnect: exponential backoff reconnect to control plane on disconnect
  • Documentation: docs/distributed.md describing shard topology and credential isolation

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 6
  • Stage 7

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Two-cluster integration test: control plane on cluster A, agent on cluster B
  • Pipeline with spec.shard: cluster-b
  • Bundle created on cluster A; PromotionStep reconciled by agent on cluster B
  • Bundle reaches Verified end-to-end
  • Agent down: PromotionStep pauses; agent restart resumes within 15 seconds
  • Credential isolation: no Git token or kubeconfig from cluster B appears in cluster A's secrets
  • --mode=standalone produces identical behavior to Stage 6 E2E test
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 15: MetricCheck CRD and Upstream Soak Time
docs.text: Goal: Add MetricCheck CRD for Prometheus-backed metric gates and implement upstream soak time in CEL PolicyGate context, completing the Phase 2 governance features.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• MetricCheck CRD type:
  • spec.provider: prometheus
  • spec.query (PromQL string)
  • spec.threshold (value, operator: lt|gt|lte|gte|eq)
  • spec.interval (how often to evaluate)
  • status.lastValue, status.lastEvaluatedAt, status.result
  • MetricCheckReconciler that:
  • Queries Prometheus via HTTP at spec.prometheusURL
  • Evaluates threshold and patches status
  • Re-queues after spec.interval
  • metrics.Provider interface with PrometheusProvider implementation
  • CEL context extension (completes the Phase 1 placeholder):
  • metrics.<name>.value — current value from MetricCheck status
  • metrics.<name>.result — Pass/Fail
  • upstream.<env>.soakMinutes — minutes since Bundle.status.environments[env].healthCheckedAt
  • Updated sample gates:
  • error-rate-gate.yaml: blocks if metrics.error_rate.value > 0.01
  • upstream-soak-gate.yaml: blocks if upstream.uat.soakMinutes < 30
  • kardinal policy simulate (extends Stage 8): now includes metric values and soak times in simulation output

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 7 (health check timestamps)
  • Stage 4 (CEL evaluator extension)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Integration test: Prometheus running in kind cluster; error-rate-gate passes when error rate < 1%, fails otherwise
  • upstream-soak-gate blocks prod promotion until uat has been healthy for 30 minutes (mocked timestamp in test)
  • kardinal policy simulate prints metric values in the simulation table
  • MetricCheck.status.lastValue updated within spec.interval of last evaluation
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 16: Custom Promotion Steps via Webhook
docs.text: Goal: Implement the custom step dispatch mechanism so teams can inject arbitrary logic into the promotion sequence via HTTP webhooks.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Custom step dispatch in PromotionStepReconciler:
  • Any spec.steps[].uses value not matching a built-in step is treated as a custom step
  • Dispatches HTTP POST to spec.steps[].webhook.url with JSON body: {bundle, environment, inputs, outputs_so_far}
  • Expects JSON response: {result: "pass|fail", outputs: {key: value}, message: string}
  • Timeout: spec.steps[].webhook.timeoutSeconds (default 300)
  • Retry: 3 attempts with 30-second backoff on transient HTTP errors (5xx)
  • Webhook authentication: spec.steps[].webhook.secretRef — Kubernetes Secret with Authorization header value
  • Step output accumulator: outputs from each step are merged and available to subsequent steps via ${steps.<name>.outputs.<key>} CEL expressions in later step inputs
  • examples/custom-step/ with a sample custom step HTTP server (Go) and Pipeline referencing it
  • Documentation: docs/custom-steps.md

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 6

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• E2E test: custom step server returns {result: "pass", outputs: {test_run_id: "abc123"}} ; next step receives test_run_id in its inputs
  • Custom step returning {result: "fail"} sets PromotionStep to Failed and Bundle to Failed with the step message as reason
  • Webhook with expired secret causes step to fail with Unauthorized reason (not retried)
  • Timeout: step exceeding timeoutSeconds is marked Failed with DeadlineExceeded reason
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 17: GitLab Support
docs.text: Goal: Add GitLab as a second SCM provider, completing the Phase 2 SCM milestone and validating the SCMProvider interface abstraction.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• pkg/scm/gitlab.go: GitLabProvider implementing SCMProvider interface using xanzy/go-gitlab
  • Provider selection: Pipeline spec.gitProvider: github|gitlab (default: github)
  • GitLab-specific PR evidence: merge request body uses same template as GitHub PR body
  • GitLab webhook: /webhook/scm detects GitLab events via X-Gitlab-Event header; validates X-Gitlab-Token
  • examples/gitlab/ with a GitLab-specific Pipeline example
  • Documentation: docs/scm-providers.md covering both GitHub and GitLab

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Stage 5

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• E2E test (GitLab.com or self-hosted): Pipeline with gitProvider: gitlab opens a merge request, evidence body is populated, merging advances the Bundle
  • Webhook validates X-Gitlab-Token; rejects invalid tokens with 401
  • GitHub provider still passes all Stage 5 and Stage 10 E2E tests (no regression)
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 18: Subscription CRD
docs.text: Goal: Implement the Subscription CRD for automatic Bundle creation from OCI registry and Git repository watching, removing the CI dependency for artifact discovery.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Subscription CRD type:
  • spec.type: image|git
  • spec.image (registry URL, tag filter regex, interval)
  • spec.git (repo URL, branch, path glob, interval)
  • spec.pipeline (target Pipeline name)
  • status.lastCheckedAt, status.lastBundleCreated
  • SubscriptionReconciler that:
  • For type: image: polls OCI registry using google/go-containerregistry at spec.image.interval; on new tag matching filter, creates an image Bundle
  • For type: git: polls Git repo at spec.git.interval; on new commit touching spec.git.path glob, creates a config Bundle
  • De-duplicates: does not create a Bundle if one already exists for the same digest/commit
  • source.Watcher interface with OCIWatcher and GitWatcher implementations
  • examples/subscription/ with OCI and Git subscription examples

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Stage 6 (Bundle creation)
  • Stage 12 (config Bundle type)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Integration test: push a new image tag to a registry; within 2 × spec.image.interval, a Bundle is created with the new tag in provenance
  • Integration test: push a commit to a Git repo touching the watched path; within 2 × spec.git.interval, a config Bundle is created
  • Duplicate prevention: pushing the same tag twice creates only one Bundle
  • make test passes

docs.title: Stage 19: Security Hardening and Production Readiness
docs.text: Goal: Complete all security non-functional requirements, add operator documentation, and prepare the project for a public pre-release.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Pod security:
  • securityContext.runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, capabilities.drop: [ALL] in all Deployment manifests
  • PodDisruptionBudget for the controller (minAvailable: 1)
  • RBAC minimization audit:
  • Review all ClusterRole rules; remove any over-permissioned verbs
  • Separate ClusterRole for org PolicyGate protection: forbids delete/patch on PolicyGates in platform-policies namespace for non-admin subjects
  • Webhook hardening:
  • Rate limiting on all webhook endpoints (token bucket, configurable via --webhook-rate-limit)
  • Structured error responses (no stack traces in 4xx/5xx responses)
  • Request size limit: 1 MB
  • Observability:
  • Prometheus metrics: kardinal_bundles_total{phase}, kardinal_steps_total{type,result}, kardinal_gate_evaluations_total{result}, kardinal_pr_duration_seconds histogram
  • Grafana dashboard JSON in config/monitoring/
  • Structured JSON logs with bundle, pipeline, env, step fields on every log line
  • Documentation completeness:
  • All docs/ files updated and cross-referenced
  • docs/troubleshooting.md covers top 10 failure modes with resolution steps
  • docs/security.md covering RBAC model, webhook auth, and credential isolation
  • Pre-release checklist: CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, GitHub release workflow in .github/workflows/release.yml

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • All previous stages

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• trivy image kardinal-promoter:latest reports zero HIGH or CRITICAL vulnerabilities
  • kubectl auth can-i delete policygate --as=system:serviceaccount:default:default -n platform-policies returns no
  • All Prometheus metrics emit under load (100 Bundles created in integration test)
  • Grafana dashboard imports without error and displays bundle phase counts
  • docs/troubleshooting.md covers: gate stuck in Pending, PR not opened, health check timeout, agent disconnected, Graph not created
  • make release (triggered by git tag) builds multi-arch images and publishes a GitHub release with signed artifacts

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Stage Description Status 0 Project skeleton, build tooling, CI ✅ Complete 1 CRD types and validation schemas ✅ Complete 2 Bundle and Pipeline reconcilers (no-op) ✅ Complete 3 Graph generation and kro integration ✅ Complete 4 PolicyGate CEL evaluator ✅ Complete 5 Git operations and GitHub PR flow ✅ Complete 6 PromotionStep reconciler and full promotion loop ✅ Complete 7 Health adapters (Deployment, Argo CD, Flux) ✅ Complete 8 CLI (full command set) ✅ Complete 9 Embedded React UI ✅ Complete 10 PR evidence, labels, webhook reliability ✅ Complete 11 GitHub Actions integration and kardinal init ✅ Complete 12 Helm strategy and config-only promotions ✅ Complete 13 Rollback and pause/resume ✅ Complete 14 Distributed mode (control plane + agents) 🚧 In Progress 15 MetricCheck CRD and upstream soak time ✅ Complete 16 Custom promotion steps via webhook ✅ Complete 17 GitLab support ✅ Complete 18 Subscription CRD ✅ Complete 19 Security hardening and production readiness 📋 Planned 20 Contiguous healthy soak + bake: shorthand (K-01) ✅ Complete 21 Pre-deploy gate type when: pre-deploy (K-02) ✅ Complete 22 Auto-rollback: ABORT vs ROLLBACK (K-03) ✅ Complete 23 ChangeWindow CRD (K-04) ✅ Complete 24 Deployment metrics on Bundle + Pipeline (K-05) ✅ Complete 25 Wave topology wave: field (K-06) ✅ Complete 26 Integration test step (K-07) ✅ Complete 27 PR review gate (K-08) ✅ Complete 28 kardinal override with audit record (K-09) ✅ Complete 29 Cross-stage history CEL functions (K-10) ✅ Complete

docs.title: How to Start Autonomous Development
docs.text: Read this. Open 5 terminal sessions. Step away.

docs.title: Prerequisites (all done)
docs.text:
• [x] GitHub repo + branch protection on main (requires 1 QA approval + CI)
  • [x] GitHub Projects board: https://github.com/users/pnz1990/projects/1
  • [x] Report issue #1 (subscribe): https://github.com/pnz1990/kardinal-promoter/issues/1
  • [x] GitHub Actions CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
  • [x] spec-kit v0.6.0 + 8 active extensions
  • [x] otherness-config.yaml (mode, CI gate, board field IDs)
  • [x] Vision, roadmap, progress, specs, and design docs in place

docs.title: The Team
docs.text:
Session Directory Role Command 1 kardinal-promoter/ Standalone agent (all roles) /otherness.run

All roles are played sequentially by the standalone agent in a single session. For concurrent scoped sessions use /otherness.run.bounded with a boundary block.

docs.title: Setup (each session, once)
docs.text:
export GH_TOKEN=<token-with-repo-and-project-scopes>

# Session 1:  export AGENT_ID="COORDINATOR"
# Session 2:  export AGENT_ID="ENGINEER-1"
# Session 3:  export AGENT_ID="ENGINEER-2"
# Session 4:  export AGENT_ID="ENGINEER-3"
# Session 5:  export AGENT_ID="QA"
# Session 6:  export AGENT_ID="SCRUM-MASTER"
# Session 7:  export AGENT_ID="PM"

git pull origin main

All sessions share the same GitHub account. The AGENT_ID badge prefixes every comment and PR review so you can tell who said what in your notification feed.

docs.title: Session Lifecycle
docs.text:
Session Runs until Coordinator All journeys ✅ Complete Engineers No more work assigned + queue exhausted QA No open PRs + coordinator posted final [PROJECT COMPLETE] Scrum Master Coordinator posted [PROJECT COMPLETE] Product Manager Coordinator posted [PROJECT COMPLETE]

All sessions loop or react continuously: - Engineers do not stop between items - QA polls for new PRs continuously - Scrum Master and PM run once per batch (triggered by coordinator [BATCH COMPLETE])

docs.title: What the Coordinator Does (automatically)
docs.text:
1. Reads docs/aide/progress.md to find the next stage
  2. Generates docs/aide/queue/queue-NNN.md (work items)
  3. Generates docs/aide/items/NNN-*.md (detailed specs per item)
  4. Populates the GitHub Projects board
  5. Creates worktrees for engineers
  6. Assigns items respecting dependency order (max 3 parallel)
  7. Tracks state in .otherness/state.json
  8. Posts batch reports to Issue #1
  9. Repeats until all 20 stages complete

docs.title: What Engineers Do (automatically, end-to-end)
docs.text:
1. Pick up assigned item
  2. Write failing tests first (TDD)
  3. Implement until go test ./... -race passes
  4. Manually validate against examples/ — kubectl apply
  5. Push PR with evidence (test output + kubectl output)
  6. Monitor CI every 3 min — fix red, re-push
  7. Poll for QA review every 5 min — fix comments, re-push
  8. Merge PR after QA approves + CI green
  9. Smoke test: go build ./... on main
  10. Pick up next item

docs.title: What QA Does (automatically, continuously)
docs.text:
1. Polls open PRs labeled kardinal every 2 min
  2. Reviews full diff against spec + user docs + examples checklist
  3. Posts request-changes with file:line references, or approve
  4. Re-reviews full diff after every engineer fix commit
  5. Escalates to Issue #1 after 3 failed fix attempts on same issue

docs.title: What Scrum Master Does (after each batch)
docs.text:
Triggered by coordinator posting [BATCH COMPLETE] to Issue #1.

  1. Reads .otherness/state.json and Issue #1 history — computes flow metrics
  2. Inspects SDLC files: sdlc.md, team.yml, templates, AGENTS.md process sections
  3. Checks: do agents actually follow the documented process?
  4. Opens sdlc-improvement Issues for systemic problems
  5. Applies minor fixes (< 10 lines) as direct PRs
  6. Posts [SDLC REVIEW] to Issue #1

Does NOT touch: vision, roadmap, journeys, user docs, code.

docs.title: What Product Manager Does (after each batch)
docs.text:
Triggered by coordinator posting [BATCH COMPLETE] to Issue #1.

  1. Checks vision alignment: do shipped features match the vision?
  2. Checks journey coverage: are journeys still the right acceptance criteria?
  3. Checks user doc freshness: do docs describe the current product?
  4. Fixes stale user docs directly via PR
  5. Opens product-gap Issues for competitor features we're missing
  6. Opens product-proposal Issues for improvements (human prioritizes)
  7. Every 3 batches: researches Kargo/GitOps Promoter/Flux releases for gaps
  8. Posts [PRODUCT REVIEW] to Issue #1

Does NOT touch: sdlc.md, team.yml, templates, code.

docs.title: Your Job
docs.text:
Board: https://github.com/users/pnz1990/projects/1 — cards move Todo → In Progress → In Review → Done

Reports: https://github.com/pnz1990/kardinal-promoter/issues/1 — subscribe to this

Act on needs-human labels: Read the issue comment. Make the decision. Remove the label. The coordinator resumes within 2 minutes.

Adjust direction: Edit docs/aide/vision.md or docs/aide/roadmap.md, then restart /otherness.run in a new session.

docs.title: If a Session Disconnects
docs.text: Restart the session in the same directory and re-run the role command. All agents read .otherness/state.json to resume. No work is lost.

docs.title: Labels
docs.text: Label Meaning Your action needs-human Agent blocked, decision needed Answer in the issue comment, remove label blocked Waiting on a dependency No action — coordinator handles report Coordinator progress update Read, no action required

docs.title: Key Files
